statement: Germany should continue to provide military support to Ukraine.
The expansion of renewable energy should continue to be financially supported by the state.
Basic income support should be cut for those who repeatedly reject job offers.
A general speed limit should apply on all motorways.
Asylum seekers who entered via another EU state should be turned away at German borders.
Rent prices should continue to be legally limited for new rentals.
Federal police should be allowed to use automated facial recognition software at train stations.
Energy-intensive companies should receive financial compensation from the state for their electricity costs.
All employees should be able to retire without reductions after 40 contribution years.
The phrase 'Responsibility before God' should continue to appear in the opening sentence of the Basic Law.
Germany should continue to promote the recruitment of skilled workers from abroad.
Germany should resume using nuclear energy for electricity generation.
The top tax rate for income taxation should be increased.
The federal government should gain more competencies in school policy.
Germany should continue to be allowed to export military equipment to Israel.
All citizens must be insured in statutory health insurance funds.
The statutory gender quota for management boards and supervisory boards of listed companies should be abolished.
Ecological agriculture should be promoted more strongly than conventional agriculture.
The federal government should increase funding for projects against right-wing extremism.
Companies should continue to be required to monitor compliance with human rights and environmental protection standards among all suppliers.
Student aid (BAföG) should continue to be paid based on parental income.
The debt brake in the Basic Law should be retained.
Asylum seekers should receive work permission in Germany immediately upon submitting their application.
Germany should abandon the goal of becoming climate-neutral.
A 35-hour work week should be established as the statutory standard working time for all employees in Germany.
Abortion should continue to be legal without punishment in the first three months only after counseling.
The euro should be replaced by a national currency in Germany.
In the expansion of transport infrastructure, rail should take priority over road.
Voluntary work should be credited toward future pensions.
Property tax should continue to be allowed to be passed on to tenants.
The right to strike for employees in critical infrastructure companies should be legally restricted.
Popular referendums should be possible at the federal level in Germany.
Children under 14 should be held criminally liable.
Germany should work toward abolishing increased EU tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles.
It should continue to be generally possible in Germany to hold German citizenship together with a second nationality.
A mandatory social service year should be introduced for young adults.
New heating systems should continue to be allowed to run completely on fossil fuels (e.g. gas or oil).
The statutory minimum wage should be increased to 15 euros by 2026 at the latest.
